events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate scenario: crew proposes shortcut for GFCI/AFCI function & troubleshooting, submittal omits product listing page and project note that would decide whether shortcut allowed. Reference NEC 210.8, 210.12. Need choose practical compliant course. Since missing listing/project note, cannot verify compatibility/listing. Shortcut not allowed without evidence. Decision likely fail or needs_more_info? The instruction: If work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Proposing shortcut without verifying listing is non-compliant (NEC 110.3(B) installation per listing, 210.8/210.12 require GFCI/AFCI protection; shortcuts could defeat protection). But maybe needs_more_info because missing submittal. However dangerous/non-compliant => fail. Need produce JSON with fields. Need fill component_conditions? none flagged but maybe non-compliant due to unverified shortcut. Work_stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 50. Risk high. Findings: shortcut proposed without product listing/project note; cannot confirm AFCI/GFCI device compatibility with OEM or installation per listing. Actions: stop work, obtain OEM listing page and project note, verify device/model compatibility, perform required function/troubleshooting per manufacturer and NEC, test per 210.8/210.12. References: NEC 110.3(B), 210.8, 210.12. Need include all fields. value 0, sound_source "", confidence 0.9, event_time 0, rate 0, order [], workable []. Ensure valid JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Crew proposed a GFCI/AFCI troubleshooting shortcut without the product listing page or project note needed to verify it is permitted for this device/installation.",
    "Cannot confirm the proposed method maintains the required ground-fault or arc-fault protection required by NEC 210.8 and 210.12.",
    "Installation and use must be in accordance with the product listing and manufacturer instructions per NEC 110.3(B)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the proposed shortcut immediately.",
    "Obtain the missing OEM product listing page and project note before proceeding.",
    "Verify the GFCI/AFCI device/model, wiring method, and troubleshooting steps are listed and permitted for this installation.",
    "Perform GFCI/AFCI function checks and troubleshooting per the manufacturer’s instructions and applicable NEC requirements.",
    "Test and document proper operation after corrective work."
  ],
  "rationale": "A shortcut that alters or bypasses GFCI/AFCI function cannot be used without proof that it is allowed by the product listing and project documentation. Without that evidence, the work is non-compliant and could leave the installation without required personnel protection.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC 210.8",
    "NEC 210.12"
  ]
}
